findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Orthonormal.lean:318` has `theorem Orthonormal.comp_linearIsometryEquiv ... : Orthonormal 𝕜 (f ∘ v)`, and `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/l2Space.lean:457`, `:463`, `:467` have `HilbertBasis.hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `tsum_inner_mul_inner`; the new proofs themselves are one-line specializations at `HilbertBasisMap.lean:91`, `:97`, and `:103`.
findings.fix: Remove these wrapper declarations and use the existing general APIs at call sites, with `simpa` where the transported basis needs to be unfolded.
findings.issue: `HilbertBasis.orthonormal_mapₗᵢ` is directly replaced by the existing general theorem `b.orthonormal.comp_linearIsometryEquiv e`; the Parseval trio at lines 88, 94, and 100 are likewise just `(b.mapₗᵢ e).hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `.summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `.tsum_inner_mul_inner` after the existing `mapₗᵢ_apply` simp rewrite.

summary: The PR adds transported Hilbert-basis consumer lemmas that are already direct specializations of existing Mathlib/TauCeti APIs rather than new reusable API.
